basic_info.description: My name's Linus. Before my time as a software engineer, I graduated with a B.S. in Psychology at University of California, Riverside. Eventually, I decided that my current path was not a proper fit for me and decided to pursue a field that revolved more around computers since that was where I was most comfortable and most versed in. I discovered software engineering and enrolled at App Academy, a rigorous 1000+ hr software engineering bootcamp. Over the course of the bootcamp, I discovered how immersive and stimulating coding could really be for and I believe my projects reflect that. Check out the some of my projects and more below!

projects.title: Limontrello
projects.description: A productivity tool inspired by Trello that helps organize your tasks for any topic. This is my most proudful project to date because of not only the styling but the functionality for dragging tasks around. The development process utilized an Agile workflow which allowed for space for flexibility and less bugs. Additionally, the drag and drop functionality was implemented through React Beautiful DnD to deliver smooth and intuitive user interactions

projects.title: Hangers Academy
projects.description: A Discord-clone made in collaboration with 3 other peers from App Academy bootcamp. We cultivated a team environment where each member was valuable and respected. We also simulated a professional Git workflow through branches and pair programming sessions which reduced debugging time and set clear expectations of various objectives.

projects.title: Overnight SnS
projects.description: My first project during my time at App Academy. This project provided the foundation of my understanding and connection between the backend and frontend we had been learning.
